title: Honest Abe's Special Day
story: Every February 12, Americans celebrate the birthday of Abraham Lincoln, the 16th President of the United States! Schools often close for the day, and many people visit Lincoln memorials or museums. Kids learn about his famous tall hat, his log cabin childhood, and how he helped end slavery. Some families read his speeches or watch movies about his life. It's a perfect day to remember "Honest Abe" and his important work for our country.
activity_question: What nickname is Abraham Lincoln commonly known by?
follow_up_question: What other presidents do you think deserve their own special celebration day?

title: Darwin Day: Celebrating Science and Discovery
story: Scientists and nature lovers around the world celebrate Darwin Day every February 12 to honor Charles Darwin, the famous scientist who discovered how animals and plants change over time. Schools host special science lessons about evolution, museums put on exhibits about fossils and wildlife, and kids get excited about learning how giraffes got their long necks or why penguins can't fly. Many people share cool animal facts on social media or visit zoos to observe different species. It's a day when curiosity rules and everyone becomes a scientist!
activity_question: What do schools often do on Darwin Day?
activity_choices: Host special science lessons about evolution
Have students dress up like dinosaurs
Cancel all classes for the day
activity_answer: Host special science lessons about evolution
follow_up_question: What animal adaptation do you think is the most amazing and why?

title: Penny for Your Thoughts on Lost Penny Day
story: Every February 12, people celebrate National Lost Penny Day by hunting for those sneaky copper coins that disappear into couch cushions, car seats, and jacket pockets! On this quirky holiday, families turn penny-hunting into a fun game, checking under furniture, in washing machines, and between car seats. Some people donate their found pennies to charity, while others start new penny collections. Kids love competing to see who can find the most lost pennies around the house. It's amazing how many pennies hide in the strangest places!
activity_question: According to the story, where do people commonly look for lost pennies on this holiday?
activity_choices: In couch cushions and car seats
activity_answer: In couch cushions and car seats
follow_up_question: What's the weirdest place you've ever found a lost penny?

title: Plum Pudding's Sweet February Surprise
story: National Plum Pudding Day on February 12 celebrates a dessert that doesn't actually contain plums! This rich, fruity cake is packed with raisins, currants, and spices instead. Families make it by mixing dried fruits, flour, and sometimes a splash of brandy, then steaming it for hours. Many people serve it warm with custard or cream, creating a cozy winter treat that brings everyone together.
activity_question: What surprising thing about plum pudding is mentioned in the story?
activity_choices: It's made with fresh fruit only
It takes only 10 minutes to cook
bonus_fact: Traditional plum pudding can be stored for months and actually tastes better with age!

title: Red Hand Day: Stop Child Soldiers
story: Every February 12, people around the world hold up red handprints to say no to using children as soldiers. Red Hand Day started when countries agreed that kids under 18 should never have to fight in wars. On this day, schools, communities, and organizations create red handprint displays by dipping hands in red paint and pressing them on posters or walls. People also wear red gloves, share red hand photos online, and sign petitions to protect children in dangerous places. The red hands remind everyone that childhood should be about learning, playing, and growing up safely, not fighting in conflicts.
activity_question: What do people create on Red Hand Day to show their support?
follow_up_question: What other ways could kids help spread awareness about protecting children around the world?

title: Digital Spring Cleaning Day!
story: Your computer is probably stuffed with old photos, forgotten downloads, and mystery files you'll never use again! National Clean Out Your Computer Day on February 12 encourages everyone to delete junk files, organize folders, and make their devices run faster and smoother.
activity_question: What does National Clean Out Your Computer Day encourage people to do?
activity_choices: Delete junk files and organize folders
Take apart their computer with tools
activity_answer: Delete junk files and organize folders
bonus_fact: Cleaning out digital clutter can free up gigabytes of storage space!

title: Georgia's Got a Special Day!
story: Georgia gets its very own celebration day on February 12! People across the state throw parties, eat delicious Georgia peaches (even if they're not in season), and share stories about their favorite Georgia places. Schools teach kids about the state's history, while families visit local landmarks and museums. Many Georgians post pictures of beautiful spots like Savannah's squares or the Blue Ridge Mountains on social media to show off their state pride.
activity_question: What fruit do people in Georgia celebrate with on Georgia Day?
bonus_fact: Georgia produces about 130 million pounds of peaches every year!
